ai_summary.summary: Senator Anthony H. Williams (D-Philadelphia/Delaware County) has been active across a range of legislative and community issues, including introducing a bill to create a hit-and-run pilot alert program in Philadelphia, passing legislation to remove licensing requirements for natural hair braiders, and co-sponsoring bipartisan probation reform that became law. He has also announced millions in state grant funding for his district and participated in hearings and task forces on topics including hospital closures, stop-and-go establishments, and public safety.

The releases cover a wide range of legislative actions, community events, and constituent resources. No single press release text was provided for analysis.

title: Telephone Town Hall
raw_text:
Telephone Town Hall
To join our next conversation, complete the form below and we will call you before the event starts. You can also come back here to listen in online.
Please note that if registering within 6 hours prior to most current event time, you may not be added to the call list until the next scheduled event.
TTH
First Name
*
Last Name
*
Email
*
Phone Number
*
Submit a Question
I hereby consent to receive autodialed and/or pre-recorded telephone calls from or on behalf of Senator Anthony H. Williams at the telephone number provided above for the purpose of participating in this Telephone Town Hall meeting.
If you are human, leave this field blank.
February 15, 2022:
Senator Williams hosts Telephone Town Hall to discuss Redistricting & Vote-By-Mail under Act 77. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/TTH-Feb-15-2022.mp3
November 23, 2020:
Senator Williams hosts Telephone Town Hall to discuss How to Manage Diabetes during the COVID-19 Pandemic. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/AHWilliams_COVID-19_Event.mp3
August 5, 2020:
Senator Williams hosts Telephone Town Hall to discuss educating children during the COVID-19 crisis. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/AHWilliams_August5_2020.mp3
June 29, 2020:
Senator Williams hosts Telephone Town Hall to discuss housing and utility assistance in the era of COVID-19. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/TTH_June29_2020.mp3
June 25, 2020:
Senator Williams hosts Telephone Town Hall on Worker Rights and COVID-19. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/TTH_June25_2020.mp3
May 18, 2020:
Senator Williams co-hosts a Telephone Town Hall to discuss mail-in voting. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
http://www.senatorhaywood.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/20200518_Haywood_COVID-19_Event.mp3
May 12, 2020:
Senator Anthony H. Williams and Senator Tim Kearney  in partnership with the Delaware County Black Caucus hosted a Telephone Town Hall meeting on the transition back to normalcy and support for the African American community in Delaware County. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatorkearney.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/20200512_DelawareCountyBlackCaucus_COVID-19_Event.mp3
May 4, 2020:
Senator Williams hosted a Telephone Town Hall to discuss the Coronavirus (COVID-19). You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/AHWilliams_COVID_May4_2020.mp3
April 14, 2020:
Senator Williams hosted a Telephone Town Hall to discuss the Coronavirus (COVID-19). You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the player below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/AHWilliams_COVID19_April14_2020-1.mp3
June 10, 2019:
Here’s Senator Williams’ latest Telephone Town Hall, during which he answers questions from callers who live in the 8th Senatorial District.  You can listen to the entire Town Hall by clicking on the link below:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/06/Sen-AHWilliams-Telephone-Town-Hall-06-10-19.mp3
March 13, 2019:
Senator Williams hosted a Telephone Town Hall, combining it with a live town hall meeting with Police Captain Scott Drissel of the 12th Philadelphia Police District. People attending the meeting live, as well as those listening on the phone, were able to ask questions of the Senator and his guest. You can hear the entire Town Hall by clicking on the link below.
Listen
February 5, 2018:
Senator Williams held another of his Telephone Town Hall meetings, in which residents of the 8th District were able to call in their questions.  Senator Williams talked with callers about local taxes, gerrymandering and stop-and-gos, among other issues.
Listen to the town hall →
June 15, 2016:
Senator Williams held another of his Telephone Town Hall Meetings last night in which participants were able to ask questions about neighborhood crime and a wide range of local and state issues as well as listen to a discussion on topics that are important to the residents of Philadelphia and Delaware counties and the Commonwealth.  You can listen here to the complete town hall meeting.
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/telephone-town-hall-meeting-ahw-06-14-2016.mp3
On Tuesday,  March 15, 2016, I held a live Telephone Town Hall Meeting which participants were able to ask questions about a wide range of topics including senior citizen issues, the cost of health care, and education as well as listen to a discussion on issues that are important to Philadelphia and the Commonwealth.
Listen here:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/telephone-town-hall-meeting-williams-03-15-2016.mp3
On Tuesday,  January 12, 2016 at 6:00 p.m., I held a live Telephone Town Hall Meeting which participants were able to ask questions about a wide range of topics including senior citizen issues, the cost of health care, and education as well as listen to a discussion on issues that are important to Philadelphia and the Commonwealth.
Listen here:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/telephone-town-hall-meeting-ahw-01-12-2016.mp3
On Tuesday, June 17, 2015 at 7:00 p.m., I held a live Telephone Town Hall Meeting on the pros and cons of legalizing marijuana in PA. Participants were able to ask me questions as well as listen to a discussion about this issue.
Listen here:
https://www.senatoranthonyhwilliams.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/senator-williams-telephone-town-hall-06-17-2014.mp3

ai_summary.summary: Senator Anthony H. Williams has hosted a series of Telephone Town Hall meetings for residents of Pennsylvania's 8th Senatorial District spanning from 2015 through 2022, covering topics such as COVID-19, redistricting, mail-in voting, housing assistance, diabetes management, education, worker rights, marijuana legalization, and local crime. The events allowed constituents to call in and ask questions directly. Recordings of each session are made available on his website.

title: Stop-and-Go Legislative Task Force
raw_text:
Stop-and-Go Legislative Task Force
About
Hearings
News
Report
About
Hearings
News
Meeting to present the report of recommendations for Section 218 of Act 49 of 2023
Download Report and Recommendations
What is the Stop-and-Go Legislative Task Force
Stop-and-Go establishments are convenience stores or delis that also sell beer and liquor. People who purchase the alcohol can consume it right outside of the establishment.
This Task Force is comprised of members of the state House and Senate, an appointee of the governor and an ex-officio member of the PLCB, that is tasked with addressing issues around stop-and-go establishments in Philadelphia.
The bi-partisan task force, authorized under Act 49 of 2023, consists Senator Williams (Chair), Senator Frank Farry, Representative Joesph Hogan, Representative Anthony Bellmon, Governor’s appointee Cornelia Swinson, and PLCB member Darrell Clark.
About
Stop and Go Establishment
is a licensee holding either a restaurant license (R) or an eating establishment license (E) when after inspection by a PLCB analyst the establishment receives violations relating to “Not a Bona Fide Restaurant and or Eating Establishment  on at least 2 occasions within a 12  month period.
Not a Bonified Restaurant is defined as follows
.
An “R” licensed establishment that fails to maintain sufficient food Items, eating utensils, dishes, seating and/or failed to provide food upon request.
An “R” licensed establishment that failed and/or refused to allow the consumption of malt or brewed beverages purchased at your licensed establishment to be consumed on the premises.
An R licensed establishment that does not have a serving area of at least 400 square feet available for the use and accommodation of the public.
Not a Bonified Eating Establishments is defined as follows.
An “E” licensed establishment that fails to maintain sufficient food Items, eating utensils, dishes, seating and/or failed to provide food upon request.
An” E” licensed establishment that does not have a serving area of at least 300 square feet available for the use and accommodation of the public.
An” E” licensed establishment where the area licensed for the sale of food is located behind a locked door not available to the public and used for the storage of malt or brewed beverages).
Establishments designated as “stop and go” licensees shall be fined in the amount of $2,500 (?)      and will immediately lose their ability to serve or sell alcohol for a minimum of 30 (?) days.
Before being allowed to reopen, a “stop and go” establishment shall enter into a compliance agreement with the PLCB, providing a written plan to correct all violations.  Such plan must be approved by the PLCB and, once approved, posted in a publicly viewable location within the “stop and go” establishment.  Returning to the sale of alcohol under a compliance plan may include provisional licensing (?)
Any stop and go establishment that fails to meet or violate the terms of the compliance agreement after inspection by a PLCB analyst, shall receive a fine in the amount of $ 5,000 (?) and immediately lose its ability to serve alcohol indefinitely pending a hearing before the ALJ to terminate license.
Within 10 days of the violation of the compliance agreement, the Licensee shall receive Notice of PLCB’s intention to permanently terminate the establishments’ ability to serve alcohol along with proof that the PLCB has requested a hearing before the ALJ.
Said administrative hearing shall take place within 30 days to determine whether to terminate establishment’s license thereby prohibiting the establishment from serving alcohol on a permanent basis.
The licensee shall have 30 days to appeal the judgment of the ALJ to the Court of Common Pleas.
The bi-partisan task force, authorized under Act 49 of 2023, consists of myself (Chair), Senator Frank Farry, Representative Joesph Hogan, Representative Anthony Bellmon, Governor’s appointee Cornelia Swinson, and PLCB member Darrell Clark.

ai_summary.summary: A bipartisan Pennsylvania legislative task force, chaired by Senator Anthony Williams, completed a year-long study of 'stop-and-go' establishments in Philadelphia — convenience stores or delis that illegally operate as bars — and voted unanimously to approve a final report of recommendations in October 2024. The report proposes a formal definition of stop-and-go establishments, fines of $2,500 for initial violations, a mandatory 30-day alcohol sales suspension, compliance agreements with the PLCB, and escalating penalties including potential permanent license revocation for repeat violations. The task force was created by Act 49 of 2023 and held multiple hearings and site tours throughout 2024.

title: Constituent Services
raw_text:
Constituent Services
My office takes great pride in assisting constituents. We will do our best to help anyone who calls, writes, emails or visits my District and Harrisburg offices.
If you have a question on any state government issue, I’ll try to answer it for you. And, if I don’t have an answer, I’ll work to get you one. If you have a problem or concern with any state government agency, please tell me about it. And, of course, your input is always welcome and appreciated on any legislative issue as I work to represent the interests of the people of the 8th District. So, please feel free to call, email, write or stop in. I look forward to hearing from you.
Check out the list of services we can help you with at anytime!
Applications and Forms
A listing of some of the commonly needed
applications and forms
available through my office and on the website.
Driver Licenses and Motor Vehicles
For help processing motor vehicle registrations, driver’s licenses, learner’s permits and other Department of Transportation forms or for assistance in solving problems involving driver’s licenses and registrations, contact any one of my offices or visit the
PA Department of Transportation Driver & Vehicle Services website
.
Legislation
To learn the status of legislation, obtain copies of House and Senate bills and state laws, contact any one of my offices or
research bills and read about legislation online
.
Touring the Capitol
Contact any one of my offices for information detailing the process for setting up school visits or other groups or
visit the Pennsylvania Capitol online
.
Senior Citizen Assistance
Contact
any one of my offices for information concerning Health Care, Septa Key Senior Photo ID Cards, State Property Tax and Rent Rebates.
Help with State Agencies
My office can assist you with problems encountered when dealing with state government and state agencies. »

title: PA Grant Directory
raw_text:
PA Grant & Resource Directory
I am pleased to provide you with this copy of the
Pennsylvania Grant Directory
, which is intended to help you, local groups and residents find grants and other resources in government.
From the Department of Aging to the state Treasury to the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Authority, the Pennsylvania Grant Directory is a great starting point for constituents and organizations seeking financial support for their work and ideas.
Some state grant programs have limited eligibility in which only municipalities, school districts and nonprofit organizations can apply. And, it is important to note that some grant, loan and assistance programs change each fiscal year. It is best to check websites or contact the appropriate agency to learn about the current status of any program.
If you need additional assistance, please
contact
my office.
Download the Pennsylvania Grant & Resource Directory →

The directory is meant to help residents, local organizations, municipalities, and nonprofits find available financial resources. The legislator is also offering their office as a point of contact for additional assistance.
